Ipomoea toxicaria is a species of flowering plant in the morning glory family, characterized by its climbing habit and trumpet-shaped flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, contributing to its potential health benefits in treating wounds, infections, and other conditions. The bioactive constituents of Ipomoea toxicaria include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Ipomoea toxicaria is used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
